title: Is Anno Trolling
wikitext:
[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:03 pm I am so sick of hearing people saying a director is "trolling" them. It's such a stupid term that only applies to fanboy/otaku culture. He is the director of the work, and therefore whatever he does is his decision as a storyteller. You can like his choice or hate it. It doesn't mean he is trolling you. If anything he doesn't give a damn about you or anyone and is just telling the story he wants to tell. OT, in my mind, anything that is not the original is an alternate universe. Just like Anima, Angelic Days (which I hate), Shinji Ikari Raising (which I hate), or all the other alternate Eva spin offs. They are all alternate realities. As in the aren't the original. Rebuild will only not be an alternate reality if it is a sequel or repeating world scenario.

[]: Post by carla » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:43 pm @nemz-- yeah, i only meant that the only definitive difference is whether or not anno ties the two continuities together in some way. until (if) that happens, the two are effectively the same thing as far as the audience is concerned. @lonecow-- i agree that whatever a director does should be a measured decision that derives from his own view of how he should tell his story and not necessarily what the audience should expect. however i also know in practice that it's hard to detach the two. it makes no sense that he'd add those little details that are not relevant to the plot if he were just doing it for himself. stuff like chalk outlines and trails of blood on the moon don't add anything for his own view of the story, if he knows they're just there as padding. they do mean something when the audience reacts to them, though, whether it be a positive or negative reaction. so it seems to me that he does take the audience into account somehow. and when he adds in stuff that he knows will confuse/misguide/bait/bother his audience, i think the "troll" shoe fits. not that i'm complaining, i love anno's trolling. i think it's genius. guess it's more of a petname in my case.

[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:48 pm carla wrote: mean something when the audience reacts to them, though, whether it be a positive or negative reaction. so it seems to me that he does take the audience into account somehow. and when he adds in stuff that he knows will confuse/misguide/bait/bother his audience, i think the "troll" shoe fits.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:28 pm I am so sick of hearing people saying a director is "trolling" them. It's such a stupid term that only applies to fanboy/otaku culture.

[]: Post by subAtomic » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:51 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You should read the interviews from the director of Revolutionary Girl Utena, or the interviews with the writer of Suzumiya Haruhi, or.......ANYTHING involving Ryukishi07. Directors and writers can and do troll the fuck out of their fanbase.

[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:52 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You should read the interviews from the director of Revolutionary Girl Utena, or the interviews with the writer of Suzumiya Haruhi, or.......ANYTHING involving Ryukishi07. Directors and writers can and do troll the fuck out of their fanbase.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:56 pm lonecow wrote: Hell if the original series came out today, all the unexplained stuff, would be called trolling by your definition.

[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 7:18 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I do believe it has been stated NGE was intended to be a slap in the face to the otaku culture.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:03 pm lonecow wrote: Yeah calling anything involving Haruhi "trolling" is another thing that annoys me. It's not trolling, it is part of their "marketing campaign". They give Haruhi a spot on the credits and any of the strange antics they pull, they can blame it on their "God" messing with it. Endless Eight totally screwed them. If you read any articles, they thought they were being clever, like they were in the original by telling the show out of order. They didn't expect it to backfire. It was them getting cocky, but they weren't trolling, they were experimenting, and it failed. But I've gotten this off topic, and I have a feeling I'm going to be the cause of another locked thread.

[]: Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:26 pm lonecow wrote: If Rebuild had come out 10 years ago, No one here would call it trolling . . . Hell if the original series came out today, all the unexplained stuff, would be called trolling by your definition. Or the last two episodes of Eva would be much bigger trolling then the sequel stuff

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:26 pm Split from here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=372073#372073 At this point, I think it's just a matter of semantics. Aside from the negative connotations of referring to stuff like this as "trolling", they're generally much the same thing. I could say: "Man, the end of the first story arc in Spice & Wolf 2 really caught me off guard." or I could say "Man, Horo (or the writers) really trolled me hard at the end of the first story arc". It's really the same thing, the second just sounds butthurt or more negative.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:34 pm Wikipedia says "In Internet slang, a troll is someone who posts inflammatory, extraneous, or off-topic messages in an online community, such as an online discussion forum, chat room, or blog, with the primary intent of provoking other users into a desired emotional response". Provoking people into a desired emotional response is what films are usually supposed to do.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:41 pm NAveryW wrote: Wikipedia says "In Internet slang, a troll is someone who posts inflammatory, extraneous, or off-topic messages in an online community, such as an online discussion forum, chat room, or blog, with the primary intent of provoking other users into a desired emotional response". Provoking people into a desired emotional response is what films are usually supposed to do.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:47 pm esselfortium wrote: It can be considered "trolling" for Anno to depict SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Jul 17, 2010 4:50 pm NAveryW wrote: Toji's "Che, I didn't get lucky" is a direct metafictional reference to the fact that he isn't the pilot this time, making the scene the exact opposite of what you described it as.

[]: Post by ran1 » Sat Jul 17, 2010 5:14 pm Oh god, this thread makes me HNNNNGH lonecow wrote: I am so sick of hearing people saying a director is "trolling" them. It's such a stupid term that only applies to fanboy/otaku culture.

[]: Post by bladerj » Sat Jul 17, 2010 9:41 pm wow....some of you are too jaded.....misguide the audience is not troll...by your definition magician and ilusionists are all trolls, by leading the audience to believe toji is the pilot the director asure the shock when Asuka is presented in his place instead,and the shock is bigger when you realise the character has a bigger role then toji ever will.

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:37 am Wow. Just wow. Looking at some of these repsonses I'm slowly becoming glad I don't visit this site as often as I used to. Some members here are so full of themselves that and arrogant it's no wonder Evageeks is not a very welcoming site to new members. Seriously, some people will disagree with you, no reason to insult them and hide behind a level of arrogant superiority.

[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Sun Jul 18, 2010 12:05 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Wow. Just wow. Looking at some of these repsonses I'm slowly becoming glad I don't visit this site as often as I used to. Some members here are so full of themselves that and arrogant it's no wonder Evageeks is not a very welcoming site to new members. Seriously, some people will disagree with you, no reason to insult them and hide behind a level of arrogant superiority.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jul 18, 2010 12:13 pm esselfortium wrote: The scene serves no purpose other than that. It's extraneous in the narrative and exists to troll the audience.
